studentEmail.non_institutional_domain: Use your institutional university email (e.g. name.surname@students.youruni.edu).

HomePage.heading: Your résumé tells what you've done. MIRA tells who you are.
HomePage.subhead: Your MiraCard lets you apply to associations and get discovered by companies on the platform. One profile that keeps opening new opportunities.
HomePage.orgEyebrow: Are you a company or a university association?
HomePage.associationBody: Apply for your association: manage applications, board members, and your MIRA page.
HomePage.companyBody: Don't wait for applications. Find the right candidates: describe who you're looking for, MIRA shows you who really fits.

guide.headerIntro: Hi {name}! Let's build your MIRA Card.
guide.headerFilled: Got it — check your details and confirm.
guide.expImprove: Write it however it comes — I'll shape it.
guide.availability: What are you looking for, and what's next?
guide.skills: From your exams, here are your skills.
guide.profile: Last thing — who you are beyond the CV.

guide.building: Here's your cycle — positions, requirements, questions.
guide.detail: AI reads every profile — you decide.

LoginPage.heading: Log in
LoginPage.pendingRequest: Your access request is still awaiting confirmation from the MIRA team. You'll get an email as soon as it's approved.
LoginPage.wrongAccountType: This is a {accountType} account. Switch to "{targetLabel}" above to sign in.
LoginPage.callbackFailed: This confirmation link was already used — this often happens because some email filters open it automatically before you do. If you received the email, your address is likely already confirmed: try logging in below.

SignupPage.heading: Create your account
SignupPage.emailHelper: Use your university email, not a personal one.
SignupPage.passwordHelper: At least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character.
SignupPage.selectUniversityAndDegree: Select your university and degree level.
SignupPage.emailSentBody: We've sent a confirmation link to {email}. Click the link to activate your account.
SignupPage.emailSentDelayNote: The email can take a few minutes to arrive — if you don't see it right away, check your spam folder too.
SignupPage.termsConsent: I have read the <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink> and I agree to the <termsLink>Terms of Service</termsLink>.

AziendePage.formIntro: Fill in your details to request access to MIRA. Our team will review the request and, if approved, send you an email to create your account.
AziendePage.contactEmailHelper: If the request is approved, you'll receive the account creation link at this address.

errors.duplicate_pending_request: You already have a pending request with this email.
errors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

landing.heading: Discover the right talent before anyone else, beyond the résumé
landing.subhead: A résumé tells you what a student has done. MIRA also shows you who they are, what they're looking for, when they're available, and how well they fit the role.
landing.pilotNote: Pilot launching September 2026. Free initial access.

step1.title: Describe what you're looking for
step1.body: Write in natural language the profile you need: role, skills, sector, attitude, availability, and much more.

step2.title: MIRA finds the matches
step2.body: The system compares your search with student profiles and shows you the most relevant candidates, with an explanation for each.

step3.title: Contact and interviews
step3.body: Send a contact request to the student. If they accept, a private chat opens where you can get to know each other and, if there's mutual interest, arrange an interview.

landing.whyHeading: Why this isn't just another recruiting portal

row1.left: You receive applications and résumés to sift through
row1.right: No job postings to publish every time.

row2.left: Information limited to what's stated in the résumé
row2.right: Profiles enriched with experience, interests, attitude, availability, and goals

row3.left: You don't really know what's behind a candidate
row3.right: Define who you want and automatically discover the best-fitting candidates.

row4.left: The student only updates their profile when job hunting
row4.right: Every match comes with an explanation of why it fits

CandidatiPage.heading: Apply for your association
CandidatiPage.intro: Fill in your association's details and create your president account. Your request will be verified within 24h. You'll receive a confirmation email. Meanwhile, you can already start building your student profile.

InAttesaPage.heading: Application pending
InAttesaPage.body: We've received your association's application. The MIRA admin will review it within 24-48 hours and you'll receive an email with the outcome.
InAttesaPage.meanwhileBody: Start building your student profile on MIRA — your MiraCard. You don't need to wait for the association's approval to do this.

steps.step4: You log in and start managing applications and the board

OnboardingFlow.transcriptReadError: I couldn't read the file — {error}. You can retry or skip.
OnboardingFlow.cvReadError: I couldn't fully read your CV: check the experiences below and fix them by hand.
OnboardingFlow.hintBocconi: You can download it from you@b: in the certificates/self-certifications section you'll find the self-certification with your exam list.
OnboardingFlow.hintGeneric: You can usually find it on your university's website or student portal, under documents and certificates.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderIntro:
Hi {name}! I'm MIRA: let's build your MIRA Card together — you need it to apply to associations, and it's the profile companies will use to find and contact you. It will be written in English, I'll take care of the translation.

Let's start with your studies: upload your transcript as a PDF and I'll fill in the fields below, exams, credits and average included. {hint} If you don't have it handy, skip and fill them in by hand.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderAfterUpload: Done: I read {courses} exams, {credits} credits{avg}. Check the fields below — fix anything you want, then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderUploaded: Your transcript is already uploaded: check the fields below, fix anything you want, then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderManual: No problem: fill in the fields below — program and years are enough, you can upload the transcript anytime from your Profile. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideEsperienze: Now your experiences. Do you have a CV? Upload it and I'll prepare them in English — then you review and fix them. If you don't have one, add them by hand below: personal projects, competitions, competitive sports, volunteering and student association activities all count — anything you think represents you. Write descriptions however they come: ✦ Improve with MIRA rewrites them in CV-style English.
OnboardingFlow.guideEsperienzeManual: Alright, let's build them by hand: add your experiences below. Personal projects, competitions, competitive sports, volunteering and student association activities all count. Write descriptions however they come — ✦ Improve with MIRA rewrites them in CV-style English. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideEsperienzeAfterCV: I found {count, plural, =0 {your experiences} one {1 experience} other {# experiences}} in your CV and prepared them below in English. Review them, fix them or add more — including the ones you'd never put on a CV (projects, sports, volunteering, association activities). Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideDisponibilita: Last thing to unlock applications: let's define your availability and next steps. Use the toggle to tell me if you're looking or open to opportunities, and fill in the fields. Below, tell me your plans for the coming months/years (exchange, graduation, Master's, work, certifications...) and whether you already have a clear direction or are still exploring — that's perfectly fine too. Write the way you speak: ✦ Improve with MIRA turns it into proper English. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideCompetenzeProposed: From your exams I've already proposed some academic skills below — confirm, fix or remove them. Then add your hard skills: tools, software, methodologies or practical abilities you can really use (if I found any in your CV, they're already in). When everything looks right, press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideCompetenzeManual: Now your skills. Academic: what you've studied and acquired in theory (e.g. financial statement analysis, accounting, statistics, marketing). Hard: tools and practical abilities you can use (e.g. Excel, Python). Add them below in English — that's the card's format. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideLingue: Which languages do you know? Add them below with level and any certification (e.g. English — C1 — IELTS 7.5). Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideLingueWithCV: I've already added the languages found in your CV below: check levels and certifications, add any missing ones, then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideProfilo:
Now the part that's usually missing from CVs: how you present yourself beyond exams and experiences. No hobby lists, no interview-style lines — tell me something authentic:
— What do you follow on your own, outside your course syllabi?
— Outside your studies, what do you do consistently?
— How would people who know you well describe you?
— What can't you stand at work or in your studies, or what would you never give up?
— What's the most "you" thing you've done in the last year?
Write the way you speak, Italian is fine too: ✦ Improve with MIRA turns it into a first-person text in English. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.gateBody: Your card is at {pct}%: you can already apply to associations on MIRA. Or we can complete the last sections in a few minutes.
OnboardingFlow.gateWarning: NOTE: The more complete your profile, the more you attract companies' attention!
OnboardingFlow.finalBody: You'll find it in the Profile section, and you can edit it anytime. Good luck with your applications.
